UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS STEVEN DILLICK AND STEPHANIE DILLICK'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES IS DENIED. DEFENDANTS ARE NOT ENTITLED TO RECOVER THEIR ATTORNEY'S FEES. THIS CASE HAS NOT BEEN RESOLVED ON THE MERITS DUE TO DEFENDANT REPRESENTATION THAT THE CASE WAS SETTLED; IT WAS NOT. THEREFORE, "THE CLAIM WAS NOT FINALLY DENIED" AND "NO ACTION [WAS] ORDERED PURSUANT TO SECTION 824(C)" DUE TO THE REPRESENTATION MADE ON THE RECORD. DEFENDANTS ARE ALSO NOT THE PREVAILING PARTIES FOR THE PURPOSES OF CCP 1717. THE CASE WAS VOLUNTARILY DISMISSED PURSUANT TO THE STIPULATION ENTERED ON THE RECORD BEFORE THE COURT. THE COURT SPECIFICALLY POINTED OUT THAT STEPHANIE DILLICK WAS ABSENT AT THE TIME OF THE SETTLEMENT BEING PUT ON THE RECORD, AND STEVEN DILLICK REPRESENTED THAT HE HAD AUTHORITY TO BIND HIS WIFE. WHETHER OR NOT SIGNED AGREEMENT BETWEEN MR. AND MRS. DILLICK EXISTED (THE ONLY OTHER LEGAL WAY TO BIND MS. DILLICK) WAS NOT WITHIN THE KNOWLEDGE OF THE COURT OR THE OPPOSING COUNSEL; IT WAS ONLY WITHIN THE KNOWLEDGE OF MR. DILLICK. THERE WAS NO MUTUAL MISTAKE AS URGED BY THE DEFENDANTS. TO THE CONTRARY, IN THE WORDS OF THE COURT OF APPEAL "WE ALSO NOTE THE CHUTZPAH OF THE DILLICKS IN MAKING [THE ARGUMENT], GIVEN STEVEN DILLICK'S EXPRESS ASSURANCE ON THE RECORD TO THE TRIAL COURT, UNCHALLENGED BY ANY COUNSEL OR THE COURT, THAT HE HAD AUTHORITY TO AGREE TO THE SETTLEMENT FOR HIMSELF AND STEPHANIE, AND STEPHANIE WOULD BE BOUND BY ITS TERMS." NO ONE CAN TAKE ADVANTAGE OF HIS OWN WRONG. CC 3517. PREVAILING PARTY SUBMITS PROPOSED ORDER IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PAUL H. ALVARADO, CLERK: M. OLOPERNES, NOT REPORTED

MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
Off Calendar May-21-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Paul D. Fife, Esq. of Wild Carey & Fife, (415-837-3101), for Jonathan Storper, Plaintiff and opposing party.
No appearance by Defendants and moving parties.

Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS STEVEN DILLICK and STEPHANIE DILLICK's MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES is DENIED. Defendants are not entitled to recover their attorney's fees. This case has not been resolved on the merits due to Defendant representation that the case was settled; it was not. Therefore, "the claim was not finally denied" and "no action [was] ordered pursuant to section 824(c)" due to the representation made on the record. Defendants are also not the prevailing parties for the purposes of CCP 1717. The case was voluntarily dismissed pursuant to the stipulation entered on the record before the Court. The Court specifically pointed out that Stephanie Dillick was absent at the time of the settlement being put on the record, and Steven Dillick represented that he had authority to bind his wife. Whether or not signed agreement between Mr. and Mrs. Dillick existed (the only other legal way to bind Ms. Dillick) was not within the knowledge of the Court or the opposing counsel; it was only within the knowledge of Mr. Dillick. There was no mutual mistake as urged by the defendants. To the contrary, in the words of the Court of Appeal "We also note the chutzpah of the Dillicks in making [the argument], given Steven Dillick's express assurance on the record to the trial court, unchallenged by any counsel or the court, that he had authority to agree to the settlement for himself and Stephanie, and Stephanie would be bound by its terms." No one can take advantage of his own wrong. CC 3517. Prevailing party submits proposed order in open court. Judge Paul H. Alvarado, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not reported (D501)
